prompt:
Create a DepotCycle Device Lifecycle Marketplace that facilitates an offline-first environment for managing device lifecycles, logistics, and secondary market interactions. The platform consists of a Leptos-based web frontend and an Axum-based backend, communicating via RESTful APIs and persisting data in a local SQLite database. 

The system supports multiple user roles: Shopper, Inventory Clerk, Manager, Support Agent, and Administrator. Users can discover listings across different campuses, manage personal favorites, and receive personalized recommendations based on their browsing history. The discovery process is enhanced with search suggestions and history tracking.

Inventory management is a core component, supporting complex workflows for receiving, issuing, transferring, returning, loaning, and scrapping devices. These workflows are documented and requires approval from Managers for sensitive actions. Real-time ledger records ensure every inventory change is traceable and immutable.

The marketplace features a robust listing system allowing sellers to post device details, condition, and pricing. Full-stack after-sales management handles returns, exchanges, and refunds with status tracking, evidence attachment, and SLA monitoring. Logistics are managed through a shipment system that tracks status transitions and ensures adherence to Service Level Agreements (SLAs).

Administrative capabilities include managing feature flags for gradual rollouts, configuring local and companion credentials for external system integration, defining templates for communication, and broadcasting announcements with audience-based delivery tracking. A centralized dashboard provides real-time metrics on marketplace activity.

Security is paramount in the offline-first design: authentication uses local username/password sessions with Argon2id hashing, session idle-timeouts, and account lockout protection. Sensitive user data is encrypted at rest using AES-256 and masked in the UI. All operations are designed to function without external network dependencies, ensuring continuity in local campus environments.
